questions.q: In which year was the All-India Muslim League founded?
questions.explanation: The All-India Muslim League was founded in 1906.

questions.q: In which city was the All-India Muslim League founded?
questions.explanation: The Muslim League was founded in 1906 at Dhaka.

questions.q: The All-India Muslim League came to represent the political interests of which community?
questions.explanation: The League came to represent the political interests of Indian Muslims.

questions.q: Which 1940 resolution of the Muslim League called for separate states for Muslims?
questions.explanation: In 1940 the League adopted the Lahore Resolution calling for separate states.

questions.q: The demand pursued by the Muslim League ultimately led to the creation of which country?
questions.explanation: The League's demand for separate states led toward the creation of Pakistan.

questions.q: Which leader is most associated with leading the Muslim League toward the demand for Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali Jinnah led the Muslim League in its demand for Pakistan.

questions.q: The 1940 resolution that set the League on the path to Pakistan is named after which city where it was adopted?
questions.explanation: The resolution is named the Lahore Resolution after the city where the League met in 1940.

questions.q: Which symbol was emblematic of the Muslim League?
questions.explanation: The League is associated with the crescent moon and star emblem.

questions.q: The Muslim League was a rival political organisation to which body during the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: The Muslim League was the principal rival to the Indian National Congress in the later freedom struggle.

questions.q: The Lahore Resolution of the Muslim League is best described as a demand for what?
questions.explanation: The Lahore Resolution demanded separate states for Muslims in British India.

questions.q: In what year did the Muslim League adopt the Lahore Resolution?
questions.explanation: The Lahore Resolution was adopted in 1940.

questions.q: The 1916 pact between the Congress and the Muslim League, agreeing on joint demands and separate electorates, is known as the what?
questions.explanation: The Lucknow Pact of 1916 brought the Congress and League together on common constitutional demands.

questions.q: Who served as the first president of the All-India Muslim League at its founding?
questions.explanation: Aga Khan III (the Aga Khan) was the first president of the All-India Muslim League.

questions.q: The Muslim League called for 'Direct Action Day' in 1946. In which city did it lead to large-scale communal violence?
questions.explanation: Direct Action Day on 16 August 1946 triggered massive violence in Calcutta.

questions.q: The two-nation theory advanced by the Muslim League held that Hindus and Muslims were what?
options: Two religious sects of one community
Two classes of the same nation
questions.explanation: The two-nation theory held that Hindus and Muslims constituted two separate nations.

questions.q: Which leader's poetic and philosophical vision of a separate Muslim homeland, expressed in his 1930 Allahabad address, influenced the League?
questions.explanation: The poet-philosopher Muhammad Iqbal, in his 1930 Allahabad address, articulated the idea of a consolidated Muslim state in the north-west.

questions.q: The name 'Pakistan', a coinage later embraced in the League's cause, is generally credited to which figure?
questions.explanation: The term 'Pakistan' is generally credited to Choudhry Rahmat Ali, a Cambridge student, in the early 1930s.

questions.q: In the 1937 provincial elections, the Muslim League's performance was best described as what?
options: An overwhelming sweep of all seats
questions.explanation: The League fared poorly in the 1937 elections, winning only a minority of Muslim-reserved seats.

questions.q: The Muslim League observed a 'Day of Deliverance' in 1939 to mark the resignation of which body?
questions.explanation: The League marked a 'Day of Deliverance' when the Congress provincial ministries resigned in 1939.

questions.q: Who moved the historic Lahore Resolution at the League's 1940 session?
questions.explanation: The Lahore Resolution was formally moved by A. K. Fazlul Huq, the premier of Bengal.

questions.q: Which reform-minded organisation, founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan, helped lay intellectual groundwork that the League's founders later built upon?
questions.explanation: The Aligarh movement of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan shaped much of the educated Muslim leadership that founded and led the League.

questions.q: Who became the first Prime Minister of Pakistan, having been a key League leader and Jinnah's chief lieutenant?
questions.explanation: Liaquat Ali Khan, a leading League figure, became Pakistan's first Prime Minister in 1947.

questions.q: On exactly what date in 1946 did the Muslim League observe Direct Action Day?
questions.explanation: Direct Action Day was observed on 16 August 1946.

questions.q: At its 1906 founding in Dhaka, the League grew out of a deputation that had earlier met the Viceroy. Who was that Viceroy?
questions.explanation: The Simla Deputation of 1906 met Viceroy Lord Minto, shortly before the League was founded at Dhaka.

questions.q: Which prominent leader closely associated with the League's founding was the Nawab of Dhaka who hosted its inaugural meeting?
questions.explanation: Nawab Salimullah of Dhaka hosted and was central to the League's 1906 founding session.

questions.q: The Muslim League initially welcomed the 1905 partition of Bengal because it created a Muslim-majority province. What was that province called?
questions.explanation: The 1905 partition created the Muslim-majority province of Eastern Bengal and Assam, which the League's early supporters favoured.

questions.q: In the 1945-46 elections, the Muslim League captured the overwhelming majority of which category of seats, strengthening its claim to speak for Muslims?
questions.explanation: In 1945-46 the League swept the great majority of the reserved Muslim seats, unlike its weak showing in 1937.

questions.q: The Cabinet Mission Plan of 1946, which the League first accepted and then rejected, proposed what structure for India?
options: A loose federation with grouped provinces
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Mission proposed a loose federation with provinces organised into groups, which the League initially accepted before withdrawing support.

questions.q: Before championing Pakistan, the League was for years dominated by landed and princely interests. Jinnah's revitalisation of it as a mass party gathered pace especially after which year?
questions.explanation: After the League's poor 1937 election showing, Jinnah rebuilt it into a mass organisation in the years that followed.

questions.q: The original 1940 Lahore Resolution spoke of 'independent states' in the plural; a 1946 League convention later amended the demand to a single state. In which city was that 1946 convention held?
questions.explanation: At the 1946 Delhi convention of League legislators, the demand was clarified into a single sovereign state of Pakistan.
